zh.lifeSummary: 當Mathilde van Limburg於大約1192年出生時，她的父親Walram van Limburg III28歲，母親Cunégonde de Lorraine21歲。她於大約1215年與Wilhelm III. von Jülich-Hengebach結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。她去世於1234年4月1日在德國諾爾德海-維斯特法倫迪倫爾利享年43歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Mathilde van Limburg于大约1192年出生时，她的父亲Walram van Limburg III28岁，母亲Cunégonde de Lorraine21岁。她于大约1215年与Wilhelm III. von Jülich-Hengebach结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。她去世于1234年4月1日在德国諾爾德海-維斯特法倫迪倫爾利享年43岁。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Limburg
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: habitational name from any of several places so named, the largest being in Hesse.

Dutch (also Van Limburg): habitational name for someone from the province of Limburg or from several places so named, like Limburg in the Belgian province of Liège or from Limbourg in Haut-Rhin, France, all named with the ancient Germanic elements lindo ‘lime tree’ or ‘dragon’ + burg ‘fortified place’.

nameMeanings.name: Mathilde
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Matilda
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

en.lifeSummary: When Mathilde van Limburg was born about 1192, her father, Walram van Limburg III, was 28 and her mother, Cunégonde de Lorraine, was 21. She married Wilhelm III. von Jülich-Hengebach about 1215. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. She died on 1 April 1234, in Jülich, Düren, North Rhine-Westphalia, Germany, at the age of 43.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Mathilde van Limburg est née environ 1192, son père, Walram van Limburg III, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Cunégonde de Lorraine, avait 21 ans. Elle a épousé Wilhelm III. von Jülich-Hengebach environ 1215. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Elle est décédée le 1 avril 1234, à Jülich, Düren, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Allemagne, à l’âge de 43 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Mathilde van Limburg um 1192 geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Walram van Limburg III 28 und ihre Mutter Cunégonde de Lorraine 21. Sie hat um 1215 Wilhelm III. von Jülich-Hengebach geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Sie starb am 1. April 1234 im Alter von 43 Jahren in Jülich, Düren, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Deutschland.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Mathilde van Limburg è nata il circa 1192, sua padre, Walram van Limburg III, aveva 28 anni e sua madre, Cunégonde de Lorraine, aveva 21 anni. Lei sposato Wilhelm III. von Jülich-Hengebach circa 1215. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì il 1 aprile 1234, nel Jülich, Düren, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Germania, all'età di 43 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Mathilde van Limburgが約1192年に生まれた時，父Walram van Limburg IIIは28歳，母Cunégonde de Lorraineは21歳であった。約1215年にWilhelm III. von Jülich-Hengebachと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼女は1234年4月1日にドイツノードルヘイン-ウエストファレンドuレンジuリチで43歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Mathilde van Limburg(이)가 대략 1192년에 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Walram van Limburg III(은)는 28세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Cunégonde de Lorraine(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 대략 1215년 Wilhelm III. von Jülich-Hengebach(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1234년 4월 1일 독일 노르드하이-w스트파렌 드u런 ju리치에서 43세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Mathilde van Limburg nasceu aproximadamente 1192, seu pai, Walram van Limburg III, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Cunégonde de Lorraine, tinha 21 anos. Ela casou-se com Wilhelm III. von Jülich-Hengebach aproximadamente 1215. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ela faleceu em 1 de abril de 1234, em Jülich, Düren, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Alemanha, com 43 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Mathilde van Limburg родилась около 1192 г., её отцу по имени Walram van Limburg III было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Cunégonde de Lorraine, 21. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Wilhelm III. von Jülich-Hengebach около 1215 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Она умерла 1 апреля 1234 г. в местности джuлич, дuрен, нордраин-вестфеилен, Германия в возрасте 43 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Mathilde van Limburg nació aproximadamente 1192, su papá, Walram van Limburg III, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Cunégonde de Lorraine, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Wilhelm III. von Jülich-Hengebach aproximadamente 1215. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Murió el 1 de abril de 1234, en Jülich, Düren, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Alemania, a la edad de 43 años.
